meaning_english: Hardworking and diligent. Synonyms: Diligent, Assiduous, Sedulous, Laborious.
Giving a lot of information clearly and in a few words. Synonyms: Succinct, Brief, Pithy, Terse.
To bring to an end. Synonyms: Cease, Conclude, Finish, Abort.
Higher in rank, status, or quality. Synonyms: Elite, Prime, Paramount, Superlative.
Full of or shedding light; bright or shining. Synonyms: Radiant, Resplendent, Lustrous, Beaming.
Lacking interest or brightness. Synonyms: Drab, Lacklustre, Somber, Tedious.
To start doing something. Synonyms: Commence, Initiate, Inaugurate, Launch.
Demanding religious thought or care. Synonyms: Solemn, Severe, Earnest, Grave.
To make a concerted or violent attack on. Synonyms: Attack, Assault, Beset, Charge.
Become aware or conscious of something. Synonyms: Discern, Notice, Distinguish, Recognize.
Decrease in vigor, power, or extent; become weaker. Synonyms: Decline, Diminish, Ebb, Dwindle.
A person who engages in a pursuit on an unpaid basis. Synonyms: Novice, Layman, Neophyte, Dilettante.
Showing or feeling no interest, enthusiasm, or concern. Synonyms: Indifferent, Unconcerned, Passive, Lukewarm.
Showing great care and perseverance. Synonyms: Meticulous, Thorough, Attentive, Persevering.
Sending out light; shining or glowing brightly. Synonyms: Effulgent, Brilliant, Incandescent, Vivid.
Not guilty of a crime or offense. Synonyms: Guiltless, Blameless, Impeccable, Virtuous.
A loud and confused noise. Synonyms: Uproar, Tumult, Din, Hubbub.
An event causing great and often sudden damage. Synonyms: Catastrophe, Disaster, Adversity, Mishap.
A strong desire to know or learn something. Synonyms: Inquisitiveness, Interest, Eagerness, Scrutiny.
Special importance or prominence given to something. Synonyms: Stress, Weight, Priority, Significance.
